meta.title: Innovation Ledger — open-design → agent-utilities (cross-domain adaptation)
meta.wire_first_heuristic: <= 3 hops from an MCP tool / FastAPI route / A2A skill; no dead code

rows.title: Declarative multi-CLI agent adapter registry + PATH auto-detection
rows.what_it_is: Each agent CLI is a declarative RuntimeAgentDef (bin, versionArgs, buildArgs(), streamFormat, promptViaStdin, listModels, fallbackModels, MCP injection). 22 defs load at boot; detection.ts probes PATH, runs --version, lists models, greps --help for capability flags, probes auth. New CLIs drop into defs/ with zero spawn-pipeline changes.
rows.novelty: Data-driven CLI behavior abstraction instead of per-CLI code; graceful degradation via fallbackModels; non-blocking eager detection cached for the picker.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: AgentAdapterRegistry mapping CLI id -> AdapterDefinition; auto-detect at startup; dispatch from core/execution/engine.py. Extends ORCH-1.2/ORCH-1.27. New concept ORCH-1.33.
rows.wiring: core/execution/engine.py:run(manifest) -> adapter dispatch (<=2 hops from /api/proxy or graph_orchestrate)
rows.success_metric: A stub CLI adapter is auto-detected and a task dispatched through it yields canonical events in an integration test; adding a new def requires no change to engine.py.

rows.title: BYOK provider-normalizing proxy with canonical SSE + DNS-resolved SSRF block
rows.what_it_is: /api/proxy/<provider>/stream routes (anthropic/openai/azure/google/ollama/senseaudio) forward {baseUrl,apiKey,model,messages} upstream and normalize each provider's stream into a uniform ProxySseEvent {type:start|delta|error|end}. validateBaseUrl + validateBaseUrlResolved DNS-resolve the host and reject RFC1918/loopback/link-local/metadata IPs (loopback carve-out for local LLMs).
rows.novelty: Daemon is a transparent provider normalizer (no per-provider SDK bundling); DNS-resolve-before-fetch closes the SSRF vector public DNS->private IP that hostname-literal checks miss.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: New FastAPI router /api/proxy/<provider>/stream + ProviderProxy in model_factory; reuse SSRF validator as a guardrail. Extends ORCH-1.27 + OS-5.3. New concept ORCH-1.34.
rows.wiring: server/app.py mounts proxy router (1 hop entry); ProviderProxy reachable from model_factory (<=2 hops)
rows.success_metric: Mock-provider request through /api/proxy/openai/stream emits canonical SSE; an internal-IP baseUrl is rejected with a 4xx before any fetch.

rows.title: Stream-protocol dispatch abstraction (streamFormat -> handler factory)
rows.what_it_is: Each adapter declares streamFormat ('claude-stream-json','acp-json-rpc','json-event-stream','plain',...). At spawn the daemon dispatches to the matching handler factory which parses protocol events and emits canonical UI events. Adding a format = new handler + one branch.
rows.novelty: Encapsulates per-protocol parsing; decouples spawn machinery from stream parsing; works uniformly for local CLI or HTTP.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: StreamHandlerFactory + format->handler map in core/execution. Extends ECO-4.x; part of ORCH-1.33.
rows.wiring: core/execution/engine.py selects handler by adapter.streamFormat (<=2 hops)
rows.success_metric: Two distinct stream formats (plain + jsonl) both normalize to the same canonical event schema in unit tests.

rows.title: Env-override model selection + 3-tier credential resolution (env > file > prompt)
rows.what_it_is: defaultModelEnvVar lets operators bake a model at the daemon level for adapters that reject 'default'. Credentials resolve env (OD_*_API_KEY) > .od/media-config.json > null, so CI/Docker, packaged installs, and GUI users each have a path.
rows.novelty: Three-tier resolution covers every deployment scenario without a separate secrets vault.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: CredentialResolver + modelOverrideEnvVar in core/config.py. Extends OS-5.x; part of ORCH-1.34.
rows.wiring: core/config.py resolver consulted by model_factory/proxy (<=2 hops)
rows.success_metric: Resolver prefers env over file over none in a unit test; modelOverrideEnvVar wins over per-call default.

rows.title: Dual prompt delivery + mid-turn tool_result injection (keep stdin open)
rows.what_it_is: promptViaStdin + promptInputFormat ('text'|'stream-json'). For stream-json the daemon writes the prompt as JSONL and leaves stdin open; on a tool_use pause it records the id in pendingHostAnswers and waits for POST /api/runs/:id/tool-result, then writes a JSONL user/tool_result line. Closes the loop on interactive tools (AskUserQuestion) in headless mode.
rows.novelty: Most adapters treat stdin as one-shot; keeping it open enables true two-way interactive tools without re-spawning.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: promptDelivery enum + /api/runs/{id}/tool-result route extending server/routers/human.py. New concept ORCH-1.35.
rows.wiring: server/routers/human.py new route -> engine resumes held run (<=2 hops)
rows.success_metric: A held-open run resumes after a tool-result POST and produces a final result in an integration test.

rows.title: Sidecar IPC + typed 5-field process stamps (socket-path isolation)
rows.what_it_is: A 5-field stamp (app, mode, namespace, ipc, source) uniquely identifies each process; sockets resolve to /tmp/.../<namespace>/<app>.sock (named pipes on Windows). The stamp is a data contract so tests spin up isolated namespaces with predictable paths and no cross-namespace delivery.
rows.novelty: Declarative multi-sidecar management without port/PID lookups; namespace isolation for parallel stacks.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: security/sidecar_runtime.py: ProcessStamp + UDS isolation per run. Extends OS-5.5/ORCH-1.11. POSIX-first; defer Windows pipes.
rows.success_metric: Two concurrent runs in distinct namespaces use distinct sockets and cannot read each other's IPC (unit test).

rows.title: Discovery-form gate (Turn-1 brief lock before work)
rows.what_it_is: A mandatory Turn-1 <question-form> locks the brief (output type, audience, tone, brand, scale) via structured radios/text before the model produces anything. RULE 1 outranks softer 'skip questions' wording in the prompt stack.
rows.novelty: Enforced at prompt-composition layer as a hard gate, not advisory.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: Pre-dispatch DiscoveryGate between router_step->dispatcher_step, reusing /api/human forms. Extends ORCH-1.0; part of AHE-3.13.
rows.wiring: graph/_router_impl.py seam (<=1 hop from orchestrate entry)
rows.success_metric: An ambiguous brief triggers a structured form before dispatch; a complete brief passes through (unit test on the gate).

rows.title: Preflight checklist gate (P0/P1/P2; no output escapes failing P0)
rows.what_it_is: Skills ship references/checklist.md with P0(blocking)/P1/P2 rules. A hard preflight rule forces reading the checklist before writing; the self-check step scores output and must fix any failing P0 before emit.
rows.novelty: Mandatory structured quality gate injected above the skill body, not a suggestion.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: harness/preflight_gate.py checked in graph/executor.py before execution. Extends AHE-3.0; part of AHE-3.13.
rows.success_metric: Output violating a P0 rule is blocked before emit; passing output proceeds (unit test).

rows.title: 5-dimensional pre-emit self-critique (score 1-5, fix <3, re-score)
rows.what_it_is: Before emit, the agent silently scores itself 1-5 on Philosophy/Hierarchy/Execution/Specificity/Restraint; any dimension <3 is a regression -> fix weakest, re-score. Two passes typical; only then emit.
rows.novelty: Quantified rubric with a forced iteration loop, not vague 'make it good'.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: MultiDimensionalCritique in harness/evaluation_engine.py, hooked into parallel_engine._synthesize before return; critique traces feed the eval/evolution engine. Extends AHE-3.1; part of AHE-3.13.
rows.success_metric: An output with a dimension <3 is re-scored/iterated before return; critique trace is persisted for AHE feedback (test).

rows.title: Prompt-stack composition with dominant-layer override
rows.what_it_is: System prompt is composed in documented priority order (identity > hard discovery rules > design-system > skill > mode-framework pinned LAST). Later layers override earlier on conflict; hard rules beat soft guidelines; infra-critical directives win all.
rows.novelty: Explicit, auditable layering prevents prompt conflicts and lets task/mode scope customize without rewriting the base.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: Formalize PromptCompositionLayer with priority/override semantics; audit which rules apply per task. Part of AHE-3.13.
rows.wiring: prompt assembly in execution path; auditable via trace (<=2 hops)
rows.success_metric: A mode-scoped directive provably overrides a conflicting base rule in a composition unit test.

rows.title: Anti-slop antipattern registry (named reject patterns + honest placeholders)
rows.what_it_is: Explicit named patterns to reject (gradient spam, generic emoji icons, invented metrics, filler copy, icon-per-heading) with the rule to use honest placeholders instead of inventing values.
rows.novelty: Names the antipatterns instead of vague 'avoid cliches'; pairs with positive direction rules.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: Store antipatterns as KG nodes (pattern + preferred alternative); consume in critique rubric + preflight. Extends KG-2.x.
rows.wiring: KG-stored registry read by critique gate (<=2 hops)
rows.success_metric: A critique flags a seeded antipattern (e.g., invented metric without source) in a test.

rows.title: Live Artifact — refreshable output (template + data.json + provenance.json)
rows.what_it_is: A live artifact = template.html + data.json rendered into index.html, schema-versioned, Mustache-style {{data.path}} + data-od-repeat (no raw HTML / no expressions), bounded JSON (8 levels/100 keys/500 items/16KiB strings/256KiB total). Refresh re-executes the source, updates data.json, regenerates if validation passes; failed refresh preserves the prior preview; snapshots in refreshes.jsonl; full provenance per generation.
rows.novelty: Separates refreshable data from fixed presentation with bounded, injection-safe interpolation and provenance.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: knowledge_graph/live_artifacts/: refresh re-derives data from KG source nodes (bi-temporal valid-time = 'failed refresh preserves prior'); provenance records producing CLI/model + KG evidence. Extends KG-2.11/2.18/2.19. New concept KG-2.24.
rows.wiring: write via mcp/kg_server.py graph_write; refresh via new gateway/api.py /api/artifacts/{id}/refresh (<=2 hops)
rows.success_metric: Create artifact -> mutate source KG node -> refresh re-derives data.json; forced refresh failure preserves prior render; provenance names model + KG evidence (integration test).

rows.title: SSRF-safe artifact contract + postMessage bridge schema (renderer deferred)
rows.what_it_is: Artifacts render via srcDoc inline injection (iframe never issues a request) with a postMessage bridge for tweaks/data, avoiding blob-URL/CORS/SSRF. UrlLoadDecision disqualifies URL-load when bridges are active.
rows.novelty: No URL exposure; data delivered in-band via messages, not query params.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: Ship the SSRF-safe artifact contract + postMessage schema as framework-native API; the iframe renderer itself is the consuming frontend's job (deferred to avoid headless dead code). Extends OS-5.3/OS-5.10.
rows.wiring: contract emitted by live_artifacts API; consumed by agent-webui externally (contract <=1 hop)
rows.success_metric: API emits a valid artifact contract + bridge schema validated against a JSON schema in a test (no renderer required).
rows.scope_note: DESCOPED: contract only; defer the iframe renderer to the frontend.

rows.title: Live progress event stream (TodoWrite-style) — event contract, not UI card
rows.what_it_is: A TodoWrite plan of short imperative items is streamed and updated in real time (in_progress before a step, completed after), pinned above the composer; progress shows X/Y.
rows.novelty: Live, non-batched progress with a stable plan snapshot.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: Ship the SSE progress event over server/routers/agent_ui.py (partly exists via StreamingResponse); drop the pinned UI card (no headless home). Extends ORCH-1.x.
rows.success_metric: A multi-step run emits ordered progress events with status transitions over SSE in a test.
rows.scope_note: DESCOPED: event contract only; UI card is the frontend's job.

rows.title: Scenario-grouped skill taxonomy + eval-scored skill picker
rows.what_it_is: Skills carry scenario (design/marketing/operation/engineering/finance/hr/sales/personal) + category + source, inferred from frontmatter or body. The picker surfaces skills grouped by scenario then category.
rows.novelty: Semantic grouping beyond filesystem; inferred scenarios auto-organize untagged skills.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: workflows/skill_picker.py with multi-factor scoring that INCLUDES prior success rate from the eval engine (self-improving), invoked from routing. Extends ECO-4.0/ORCH-1.2/AHE-3.1. No new concept.
rows.success_metric: Picker ranks a higher-historical-success skill above a keyword-equal rival; scenario filter narrows candidates (test).

rows.title: Self-contained SKILL.md manifest with per-skill critique-policy override
rows.what_it_is: Each skill is one SKILL.md (YAML frontmatter: name, description, triggers, scenario, category, od.critique.policy required|opt-out|opt-in|null, requires) + freeform body read verbatim; skills can shadow built-ins by id; i18n fields supported.
rows.novelty: Version-controllable file-based capability format with per-skill critique rollout override and shadowing.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: Align skill_compiler frontmatter to carry scenario/category/critique-policy; critique gate honors the per-skill override. Extends ECO-4.x.
rows.wiring: workflows/skill_compiler.py frontmatter parse -> critique gate reads policy (<=2 hops)
rows.success_metric: A skill with critique.policy=opt-out bypasses the critique gate; required forces it (test).

rows.title: Unified dev-lifecycle CLI (start/stop/status/logs/inspect/run, --namespace, --json)
rows.what_it_is: One CLI controls daemon/web/desktop lifecycle with sidecar stamps into a shared namespace; all state under .tmp/<namespace>/...; --namespace runs parallel isolated stacks; --json for CI.
rows.novelty: Single point of control replacing scattered scripts; namespace isolation for parallel instances.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: New agent-utilities console-script with start/stop/status/logs/inspect/run + --namespace + --json. Extends OS-5.1/5.2.
rows.success_metric: `agent-utilities status --json` reports component states; two --namespace stacks don't collide (smoke test).

rows.title: Ubiquitous-language CONTEXT.md glossary with CI sync check
rows.what_it_is: A versioned domain glossary: each term has a single source-of-truth definition + 'Avoid:' guidance + relationship cardinalities; used to prevent terminology drift in code/PRs/docs.
rows.novelty: Domain glossary as a CI-checked repo artifact, complementing the concept registry.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: docs/CONTEXT.md glossary (skills/scenarios/KG/orchestration roles) with a CI sync check against concepts.yaml. Complements existing registry.
rows.success_metric: CI fails when a defined term's 'Avoid' synonym appears in new docs/code (lint test).

rows.title: Run-scoped tool token (runId/project/endpoint/expiry; daemon = sole policy authority)
rows.what_it_is: Each run gets a short-lived OD_TOOL_TOKEN bound to runId, projectId, allowed endpoints/operations, expiry, injected into the agent env. Tool endpoints derive scope from the token and reject request-supplied project overrides; revoked at run end/TTL.
rows.novelty: Run-scoped authorization without per-agent auth setup; token encodes the allowlist; daemon is the single policy authority.
rows.adapt_to_agent_utilities: Extend security/brain_context.py ActorContext + tool_guard with a RunScopedToken minted/injected by the dev CLI's run command. New concept OS-5.11.
rows.wiring: engine dispatch mints token; tool_guard validates (<=2 hops)
rows.success_metric: A tool call with an expired/out-of-scope token is rejected by tool_guard; an in-scope call passes (test).

deferred.reason: iframe renderer is a frontend concern; keep contract, defer renderer
pinned UI card has no headless home; ship SSE event only
Windows named-pipe parity deferred; POSIX sockets first
